# General Information

## Maintainer
Oliver Bründler [oliver.bruendler@psi.ch]

## License
This library is published under [PSI HDL Library License](License.txt), which is [LGPL](LGPL2_1.txt) plus some additional exceptions to clarify the LGPL terms in the context of firmware development.

## Detailed Documentation
For details, refer to the [Documentation](doc/Documentation.md)

## Changelog
See [Changelog](Changelog.md)

<!-- DO NOT CHANGE FORMAT: this section is parsed to resolve dependencies -->

# Dependencies

The required folder structure looks as given below (folder names must be matched exactly). 

Alternatively the repository [psi\_fpga\_all](https://github.com/paulscherrerinstitute/psi_fpga_all) can be used. This repo contains all FPGA related repositories as submodules in the correct folder structure.

* TCL
  * [PsiSim](https://github.com/paulscherrerinstitute/PsiSim) (2.4.0 or higher, for development only)
  * [PsiIpPackage](https://github.com/paulscherrerinstitute/PsiIpPackage) (2.2.0, for development only )
* VHDL
  * [psi\_common](https://github.com/paulscherrerinstitute/psi_common) (2.10.0 or higher)
  * [psi\_tb](https://github.com/paulscherrerinstitute/psi_tb) (2.5.0 or higher, for development only)
* VivadoIp
  * [**vivadoIP\_axi\_mm\_reader**](https://github.com/paulscherrerinstitute/vivadoIP_axi_mm_reader)
  
<!-- END OF PARSED SECTION -->
  
Dependencies can also be checked out using the python script *scripts/dependencies.py*. For details, refer to the help of the script:

```
python dependencies.py -help
```

Note that the [dependencies package](https://github.com/paulscherrerinstitute/PsiFpgaLibDependencies) must be installed in order to run the script.

# Description
This IP-core reads a number of registers automatically (periodic or upon an event) and makes them available to SW through a FIFO or transmits them through an AXI-Stream interface.

# Simulations and Testbenches

A regression test script for Modelsim is present. To run the regression test, execute the following command in modelsim from within the directory *sim*

```
source ./run.tcl
``` 

If you are using GHDL:

```
source ./runGhdl.tcl
```

content.content:
1 # AXI MM Reader IP-Core Documentation 2 3 ## Overview 4 5 This IP-Core allows reading a list of 32-bit registers over AXI-4 periodically or upon trigger events. The registers read can either be read by software from an internal FIFO or they are transmitted through an AXI-Stream interface. Which option is used, is configurable in the GUI. 6 7 The register addresses to be read are defined in a small RAM. For every read cycle, the IP-Core iterates through this table and reads all the registers configuredc. The values read are then made available through the interface configured. 8 9 The intended usages of the IP-Core are: 10 * Converting values that are only accessible through registers (e.g. temperature/voltage of the System-Monitor) to AXI-Stream 11 * Get state of status registers at exact points in time (compared to reading by SW which always has quite a lot of jitter) 12 13 ## License 14 This library is published under [PSI HDL Library License](License.txt), which is [LGPL](LGPL2_1.txt) plus some additional exceptions to clarify the LGPL terms in the context of firmware development. 15 16 ## IP Integration 17 18 ### Interfaces 19 20 The figure below shows the IP as showing up in Vivado IPI. 21 22 ![ipi-component](./pics/ipi-component.png) 23 24 * The IP-Core has a common clock/reset for all interfaces. If different clocks are required, clock-crossing components must be added externally. 25 * Whenever a pulse on the trigger inputs occurs, the IP-core executes a register read cycle. 26 * Trigger input pulses while a read cycle is still going on are ignored 27 * Only use pulses as inputs (exactly one high-cycle) 28 * The *s00\_axi* AXI interface is used to configure the registers of the IP-Core 29 * The *m00\_axi* Interface is used to read the registers configured 30 * DoneIrq pulses (one high-cycle) when a read cycle was ompleted 31 * The *m\_axis* port is present or not depending on the IP-Configuration 32 * If register values are transmitted over AXI-Stream, this port is present 33 * If register values are read via register bank from the internal FIFO, this port is not present 34 35 ### Configuration GUI 36 37 The figure below shows the configuration GUI: 38 39 ![ipi-gui](./pics/ipi-gui.png) 40 41 The parameters are briefly described here: 42 * **Address Width of the s00_axi interface** 43 * The address width must be set to at least **_ceil(log2(MaxRegisters x 4 + 32))_** 44 * **Clock frequency in Hz** 45 * The clock frequency is required to calculate the timeout (see below) 46 * **Timeout in us** 47 * If the core is enabled (see register bank description) and no trigger inputs arrive, a read-cycle is started after this time. This functionality can also be used to configure periodic reads by applying a timeout and not using the trigger input. 48 * **Maximum number of registers to read for each cycle** 49 * Number of entries in the configuration ROM. The number of registers to read can be configured at runtime to be smaller than the maximum number of registers. 50 * **Buffer space for this number of read cycles is reserved** 51 * The IP-Core contains a FIFO for buffering register read-values. The number of complete read cycles to be buffered in these FIFOs can be set here. In the default configuration (see screenshot), up to 4096 register values (4 x 1024) can be buffered in this FIFO. Hence SW has to react within four read-cycles to not loose any data. This option is especially useful if SW is receiving the read data and real-time performance cannot be guaranteed (e.g. because the SW is running on Linux). 52 * **Output type** 53 * AXIS - Register read values are transmitted over an AXI-S interface 54 * AXIMM - Register read values are received by SW over a FIFO that is mapped to the register space of the IP-Core 55 56 ## Register Bank 57 58 ### Register Modes 59 60 | Mode | Description | 61 |:----:|:-------------------------| 62 | R | Read only | 63 | W | Write only | 64 | RW | Read/write | 65 | RV | Read with side effect | 66 | RCW1 | Ready, clear by wrigin 1 | 67 68 ### Registers 69 70 | Address | Name | Mode | Bits | Description | 71 |:-------:|:------:|:----:|:----:|:------------| 72 | 0x00 | Ena | RW | 0 | Enable IP-Core | 73 | 0x04 | RegCnt | RW | 31:0 | Number of registers to read (first N entries in the *RegTable* are used).
Do not change while IP is enabled. | 74 | 0x08 | RdData | RV | 31:0 | Register read value FIFO. Reading this register removes the entry read from the FIFO.
Only present for *Output type = AXIMM* | 75 | 0x0C | RdLast | R | 0 | 1 = current value in *RdData* is the last of a read-cycle
Only present for *Output type = AXIMM* | 76 | 0x10 | Level | R | 31:0 | Level of the internal read value buffer FIFO | 77 | 0x20 | Addr[0] | RW | 31:0 | Address of first register to read | 78 | 0x24 | Addr[1] | RW | 31:0 | Address of second register to read | 79 | ... | ... | ... |.... | ... | 80 81 82 When operating in *AXIMM* output mode, the *RdLast* must be read before *RdData* because reading *RdData* removes the entry from the FIFO. 83 84 ## Architecture 85 86 The figure below shows the overall architecture if the IP-Core. 87 88 ![architecture](./pics/architecture.png) 89 90

The register addresses to be read are defined in a small RAM. For every read cycle, the IP-Core iterates through this table and reads all the registers configuredc. The values read are then made available through the interface configured. The intended usages of the IP-Core are: * Converting values that are only accessible through registers (e.g. temperature/voltage of the System-Monitor) to AXI-Stream * Get state of status registers at exact points in time (compared to reading by SW which always has quite a lot of jitter) ## License This library is published under [PSI HDL Library License](License.txt), which is [LGPL](LGPL2_1.txt) plus some additional exceptions to clarify the LGPL terms in the context of firmware development. ## IP Integration ### Interfaces The figure below shows the IP as showing up in Vivado IPI. ![ipi-component](./pics/ipi-component.png) * The IP-Core has a common clock/reset for all interfaces. If different clocks are required, clock-crossing components must be added externally. * Whenever a pulse on the trigger inputs occurs, the IP-core executes a register read cycle. * Trigger input pulses while a read cycle is still going on are ignored * Only use pulses as inputs (exactly one high-cycle) * The *s00\_axi* AXI interface is used to configure the registers of the IP-Core * The *m00\_axi* Interface is used to read the registers configured * DoneIrq pulses (one high-cycle) when a read cycle was ompleted * The *m\_axis* port is present or not depending on the IP-Configuration * If register values are transmitted over AXI-Stream, this port is present * If register values are read via register bank from the internal FIFO, this port is not present ### Configuration GUI The figure below shows the configuration GUI: ![ipi-gui](./pics/ipi-gui.png) The parameters are briefly described here: * **Address Width of the s00_axi interface** * The address width must be set to at least **_ceil(log2(MaxRegisters x 4 + 32))_** * **Clock frequency in Hz** * The clock frequency is required to calculate the timeout (see below) * **Timeout in us** * If the core is enabled (see register bank description) and no trigger inputs arrive, a read-cycle is started after this time. This functionality can also be used to configure periodic reads by applying a timeout and not using the trigger input. * **Maximum number of registers to read for each cycle** * Number of entries in the configuration ROM. The number of registers to read can be configured at runtime to be smaller than the maximum number of registers. * **Buffer space for this number of read cycles is reserved** * The IP-Core contains a FIFO for buffering register read-values. The number of complete read cycles to be buffered in these FIFOs can be set here. In the default configuration (see screenshot), up to 4096 register values (4 x 1024) can be buffered in this FIFO. Hence SW has to react within four read-cycles to not loose any data. This option is especially useful if SW is receiving the read data and real-time performance cannot be guaranteed (e.g. because the SW is running on Linux). * **Output type** * AXIS - Register read values are transmitted over an AXI-S interface * AXIMM - Register read values are received by SW over a FIFO that is mapped to the register space of the IP-Core ## Register Bank ### Register Modes | Mode | Description | |:----:|:-------------------------| | R | Read only | | W | Write only | | RW | Read/write | | RV | Read with side effect | | RCW1 | Ready, clear by wrigin 1 | ### Registers | Address | Name | Mode | Bits | Description | |:-------:|:------:|:----:|:----:|:------------| | 0x00 | Ena | RW | 0 | Enable IP-Core | | 0x04 | RegCnt | RW | 31:0 | Number of registers to read (first N entries in the *RegTable* are used).
Do not change while IP is enabled. | | 0x08 | RdData | RV | 31:0 | Register read value FIFO. Reading this register removes the entry read from the FIFO.
Only present for *Output type = AXIMM* | | 0x0C | RdLast | R | 0 | 1 = current value in *RdData* is the last of a read-cycle
Only present for *Output type = AXIMM* | | 0x10 | Level | R | 31:0 | Level of the internal read value buffer FIFO | | 0x20 | Addr[0] | RW | 31:0 | Address of first register to read | | 0x24 | Addr[1] | RW | 31:0 | Address of second register to read | | ... | ... | ... |.... | ... | When operating in *AXIMM* output mode, the *RdLast* must be read before *RdData* because reading *RdData* removes the entry from the FIFO. ## Architecture The figure below shows the overall architecture if the IP-Core. ![architecture](./pics/architecture.png)

##############################################################################

###############################################################
# Include PSI packaging commands
###############################################################
source ../../../TCL/PsiIpPackage/PsiIpPackage.tcl
namespace import -force psi::ip_package::latest::*

###############################################################
# General Information
###############################################################
set IP_NAME axi_mm_reader
set IP_VERSION 1.0
set IP_REVISION "auto"
set IP_LIBRARY PSI
set IP_DESCIRPTION "AXI-MM register readout (periodic readout)"

init $IP_NAME $IP_VERSION $IP_REVISION $IP_LIBRARY
set_description $IP_DESCIRPTION
set_vendor "Oliver Bründler"
set_vendor_short "oliver.bruendler"
set_vendor_url "https://none.com"
set_logo_relative "../doc/LogoRhino.png"
set_datasheet_relative "../doc/index.html"

###############################################################
# Add Source Files
###############################################################

#Relative Source Files
add_sources_relative { \
	../hdl/definitions_pkg.vhd \
	../hdl/axi_mm_reader.vhd \
	../hdl/axi_mm_reader_wrp.vhd \
}

#PSI Common
add_lib_relative \
	"../../../VHDL/psi_common/hdl"	\
	{ \
		psi_common_array_pkg.vhd \
		psi_common_math_pkg.vhd \
		psi_common_sdp_ram.vhd  \
		psi_common_sync_fifo.vhd \
		psi_common_logic_pkg.vhd \
		psi_common_pl_stage.vhd \
		psi_common_axi_slave_ipif.vhd \
		psi_common_axi_master_simple.vhd \
		psi_common_tdp_ram.vhd \
	}

###############################################################
# Driver Files
###############################################################	

add_drivers_relative ../drivers/axi_mm_reader { \
	src/axi_mm_reader.c \
	src/axi_mm_reader.h \
}
	

###############################################################
# GUI Parameters
###############################################################

#User Parameters
gui_add_page "Configuration"

gui_create_parameter "AxiSlaveAddrWidth_g" "Address with of the s00_axi interface"
gui_parameter_set_range 8 24
gui_add_parameter

gui_create_parameter "ClkFrequencyHz" "Clock frequency in Hz"
gui_add_parameter

gui_create_parameter "TimeoutUs_g" "Timeout in us (automatically start reading if not trigger arrives for this time)"
gui_parameter_set_range 1 10000
gui_add_parameter

gui_create_parameter "MaxRegCount_g" "Maximum number or registers to read for each cycle"
gui_add_parameter

gui_create_parameter "MinBuffers_g" "Buffer space for this number of read cycles is reserved"

gui_add_parameter

gui_create_parameter "Output_g" "Output type (read from registers or transmit via AXI-S)"
gui_parameter_set_widget_dropdown {"AXIMM" "AXIS"}
gui_add_parameter


###############################################################
# Remove wrongly detected interfaces
###############################################################
remove_autodetected_interface Rst

###############################################################
# Optional Ports
###############################################################

add_port_enablement_condition m_axis_* "\$Output_g == \"AXIS\""
add_interface_enablement_condition m_axis "\$Output_g == \"AXIS\""

###############################################################
# Package Core
###############################################################
set TargetDir ".."
#											Edit  	Synth	Part
package_ip $TargetDir 						false 	true	xc7a*

##############################################################################
#  Copyright (c) 2019 by Paul Scherrer Institute, Switzerland
#  All rights reserved.
#  Authors: Oliver Bruendler
##############################################################################

import os

THIS_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))

os.chdir(THIS_DIR + "/../sim")

os.system("vsim -c -do ci.do")

with open("Transcript.transcript") as f:
	content = f.read()
	
#Expected Errors
if "###ERROR###" in content:
	exit(-1)
#Unexpected Errors
if "SIMULATIONS COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY" not in content:
	exit(-2)
	
	
#Success
exit(0)

I now have a thorough understanding of the entire project. Let me design the learning handbook.

## Project Summary

**vivadoIP_axi_mm_reader** is a Xilinx Vivado IP core developed by Paul Scherrer Institute (PSI) / Oliver Bründler. It automatically reads a list of 32-bit registers over AXI4 memory-mapped (M00_AXI master) periodically or upon a trigger event, and delivers the values either:
- via an AXI-Stream (m_axis) interface, OR
- through a software-accessible FIFO mapped into the IP's register space (s00_axi slave).

Key components:
1. **hdl/axi_mm_reader.vhd** — core RTL with a 2-process (combinatorial + sequential) state machine (FSM: Idle, ReadAddr, SetCmd, ApplyCmd, WaitDone). Uses psi_common_tdp_ram (register config table) and psi_common_sync_fifo (read value buffer).
2. **hdl/definitions_pkg.vhd** — register address constants (RegIdx_Ctrl, RegIdx_RegCnt, RegIdx_RdData, RegIdx_RdLast, RegIdx_Level, MemOffs).
3. **hdl/axi_mm_reader_wrp.vhd** — the wrapper that wraps the core with:
   - psi_common_axi_slave_ipif (AXI4-Lite-ish slave for register/memory config)
   - psi_common_axi_master_simple (AXI4 master for register reads)
   - generate blocks for AXIS vs AXIMM output mode
4. **tb/top_tb.vhd** — testbench with 6 stimulus cases (single read, buffered double read, timeout, disabled, back pressure, single reg 4x), generic OutputType_g for both output modes.
5. **drivers/axi_mm_reader/** — C driver for Xilinx Vitis/Vivado SDK using Xil_In32/Xil_Out32. Header axi_mm_reader.h, impl axi_mm_reader.c, Makefile, MDD, tcl.
6. **sim/** — PsiSim-based simulation config (config.tcl, run.tcl, ci.do, GHDL variant).
7. **scripts/package.tcl** — PsiIpPackage-based IP packaging into Vivado IP.
8. **xgui/axi_mm_reader_v1_0.tcl** — GUI parameter definitions.
9. **bd/bd.tcl** — Block Design hooks for AXI ID_WIDTH propagation.

input.rationale: 本项目是一个由 Paul Scherrer Institute (PSI) / Oliver Bründler 开发的 Xilinx Vivado IP 核 vivadoIP_axi_mm_reader，功能是周期性或由事件触发地经 AXI4 总线读取一批 32 位寄存器，并把结果经 AXI-Stream 或软件 FIFO 输出。学习路径设计为三层：第一单元（入门）先让读者认识项目定位、目录结构、仿真与打包流程，建立“如何看懂并跑起来一个 Vivado IP”的整体认知；第二单元（进阶）自顶向下拆解 wrapper → 寄存器映射 → 核心 FSM → 触发/超时 → AXI 从机 → AXI 主机 → 输出模式与 FIFO 的数据通路，是理解硬件实现的主干；第三单元（专家）覆盖 C 软件驱动、仿真测试台、参数化与 GUI、IP-XACT 打包与 Block Design 集成，以及二次开发实践，帮助读者从“看懂”走向“改造”。讲义之间通过 depends_on 形成依赖图，保证读者可按顺序由浅入深地结合真实源码学习。

units.title: 入门：认识项目、目录与运行方式

lectures.title: 项目概览：vivadoIP_axi_mm_reader 是什么
lectures.topic: 介绍该 IP 核的功能定位：周期性或触发式地经 AXI4 读取一批 32 位寄存器，并经 AXI-Stream 或软件 FIFO 输出；说明典型用途、许可证（PSI HDL/LGPL）与外部依赖。
learning_goals: 理解 IP 核的功能定位与典型应用场景（如把 System-Monitor 寄存器转成 AXI-Stream、低抖动采集状态寄存器）
了解许可证（PSI HDL Library License / LGPL）与依赖关系（psi_common、PsiSim、PsiIpPackage、psi_tb）
建立后续学习所需的背景知识与项目动机
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 README 与 Documentation，用自己的话写出该 IP 核解决什么问题、有哪些可配置项与两种输出方式，并列出运行仿真与打包所需的外部依赖。

lectures.title: 仓库目录结构速览
lectures.topic: 梳理仓库目录布局：hdl（核心与 wrapper）、tb（测试台）、sim（仿真脚本）、bd/xgui/scripts（打包与 GUI）、drivers（C 驱动）、doc 及 component.xml 各自的职责。
learning_goals: 掌握仓库目录的整体布局
理解每个目录在 IP 开发流程（编码→仿真→打包→驱动）中的角色

lectures.practice_task: 绘制仓库目录树并标注每个目录的职责，指出“核心 RTL、wrapper、测试台、C 驱动入口”分别位于哪些具体文件。

lectures.title: 如何运行仿真与 CI 流程
lectures.topic: 讲解基于 PsiSim 的仿真流程：config.tcl 的源文件分组（lib/src/tb）、run.tcl 的编译与运行、Modelsim 的 source ./run.tcl、GHDL 的 runGhdl.tcl，以及 ci.do/ciFlow.py 如何解析 transcript 判定通过。
learning_goals: 理解 PsiSim 仿真库的配置与源文件分组（lib/src/tb tag）

lectures.practice_task: 梳理一次完整的仿真运行步骤；说明若要新增一个测试台文件需修改 config.tcl 的哪几处；并解释 ciFlow.py 中 “###ERROR###” 与 “SIMULATIONS COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY” 两个判据各自的作用。

lectures.title: IP 打包与 Vivado 集成
lectures.topic: 讲解如何用 PsiIpPackage（scripts/package.tcl）把 HDL 打包成 Vivado IP：加入源文件与 psi_common 库、声明 GUI 参数页、设置 m_axis 可选端口的启用条件、目标器件与综合设置。
learning_goals: 理解 package.tcl 的打包流程与 PsiIpPackage 命令序列

lectures.practice_task: 阅读 package.tcl，写出一个 IP 打包步骤清单（初始化→加源→加库→加驱动→加 GUI 参数→设置可选端口→package_ip），并解释为何 m_axis 端口只在 Output_g=="AXIS" 时出现。

units.title: 进阶：核心模块与数据通路

lectures.title: 整体架构与数据流
lectures.topic: 自顶向下讲解 IP 核整体架构与数据流：s00_axi（从机，配置）→ RegTable RAM → 核心 FSM → m00_axi（主机，读寄存器）→ FIFO → AXIS 或 AXIMM 输出，结合 Documentation 的架构图。
learning_goals: 建立完整数据通路的心智模型

lectures.practice_task: 用表格列出所有寄存器的地址、名称、模式、位宽与用途；并指出 AXIMM 模式下为何“读 RdData 会弹出 FIFO、因此要先读 RdLast”。

lectures.title: 核心 FSM：双进程状态机
lectures.topic: 深入 axi_mm_reader.vhd 的核心状态机：双进程（two-process）方法、状态 Idle/ReadAddr/SetCmd/ApplyCmd/WaitDone 的转换与每态动作、DoneCnt 计数与 Last 检测。

lectures.practice_task: 画出 FSM 状态转移图并标注转移条件；解释在 ApplyCmd_s 等待 AxiM_CmdRd_Rdy 时 RamAddr 已经自增到下一项的设计意图。

lectures.title: 触发与超时机制
lectures.topic: 讲解读周期启动机制：Trig 脉冲触发与 TimeoutCkCycles_g 超时自动触发、Enable 的门控、超时计数器的复位以及如何用超时实现周期性读取。
learning_goals: 理解触发与超时两种启动方式

理解驱动如何在 Vitis BSP 中被识别并生成 xparameters.h
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 bd.tcl，说明 pre_propagate（针对 master）与 propagate（针对 slave）分别在什么时机修改 ID_WIDTH；并追踪 axi_mm_reader.tcl 的 generate 过程会向 xparameters.h 写入哪些字段（NUM_INSTANCES/DEVICE_ID/C_BASEADDR/C_HIGHADDR）。

lectures.title: 二次开发实践：扩展该 IP
lectures.topic: 二次开发实践：如何端到端扩展该 IP——新增配置寄存器（definitions_pkg + wrapper 解码 + 文档）、调整输出/缓冲深度、为新增行为编写测试用例并加入 sim/config.tcl、同步更新 C 驱动。
learning_goals: 掌握从寄存器定义到硬件/文档/测试/驱动的端到端改动流程
理解一次扩展需要同步修改的多处文件与潜在风险
具备安全扩展 IP（含仿真验证）的能力
lectures.practice_task: 设计一个“读取内部状态”的小扩展（例如新增一个只读寄存器反映 DoneCnt/Level），写出需要修改的文件清单与每个文件的关键改动点，并在 top_tb 中规划一个验证用例、在 config.tcl 中确认无需新增文件。

data.head: ca5ef76b35221949ccac652c9e97978268dd956f
data.rationale: 本项目是一个由 Paul Scherrer Institute (PSI) / Oliver Bründler 开发的 Xilinx Vivado IP 核 vivadoIP_axi_mm_reader，功能是周期性或由事件触发地经 AXI4 总线读取一批 32 位寄存器，并把结果经 AXI-Stream 或软件 FIFO 输出。学习路径设计为三层：第一单元（入门）先让读者认识项目定位、目录结构、仿真与打包流程，建立“如何看懂并跑起来一个 Vivado IP”的整体认知；第二单元（进阶）自顶向下拆解 wrapper → 寄存器映射 → 核心 FSM → 触发/超时 → AXI 从机 → AXI 主机 → 输出模式与 FIFO 的数据通路，是理解硬件实现的主干；第三单元（专家）覆盖 C 软件驱动、仿真测试台、参数化与 GUI、IP-XACT 打包与 Block Design 集成，以及二次开发实践，帮助读者从“看懂”走向“改造”。讲义之间通过 depends_on 形成依赖图，保证读者可按顺序由浅入深地结合真实源码学习。

units.title: 入门：认识项目、目录与运行方式

lectures.title: 项目概览：vivadoIP_axi_mm_reader 是什么
lectures.topic: 介绍该 IP 核的功能定位：周期性或触发式地经 AXI4 读取一批 32 位寄存器，并经 AXI-Stream 或软件 FIFO 输出；说明典型用途、许可证（PSI HDL/LGPL）与外部依赖。
learning_goals: 理解 IP 核的功能定位与典型应用场景（如把 System-Monitor 寄存器转成 AXI-Stream、低抖动采集状态寄存器）
了解许可证（PSI HDL Library License / LGPL）与依赖关系（psi_common、PsiSim、PsiIpPackage、psi_tb）
建立后续学习所需的背景知识与项目动机
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 README 与 Documentation，用自己的话写出该 IP 核解决什么问题、有哪些可配置项与两种输出方式，并列出运行仿真与打包所需的外部依赖。

lectures.title: 仓库目录结构速览
lectures.topic: 梳理仓库目录布局：hdl（核心与 wrapper）、tb（测试台）、sim（仿真脚本）、bd/xgui/scripts（打包与 GUI）、drivers（C 驱动）、doc 及 component.xml 各自的职责。
learning_goals: 掌握仓库目录的整体布局
理解每个目录在 IP 开发流程（编码→仿真→打包→驱动）中的角色
能够快速定位核心源码、wrapper、测试台与驱动入口
lectures.practice_task: 绘制仓库目录树并标注每个目录的职责，指出“核心 RTL、wrapper、测试台、C 驱动入口”分别位于哪些具体文件。

lectures.title: 如何运行仿真与 CI 流程
lectures.topic: 讲解基于 PsiSim 的仿真流程：config.tcl 的源文件分组（lib/src/tb）、run.tcl 的编译与运行、Modelsim 的 source ./run.tcl、GHDL 的 runGhdl.tcl，以及 ci.do/ciFlow.py 如何解析 transcript 判定通过。
learning_goals: 理解 PsiSim 仿真库的配置与源文件分组（lib/src/tb tag）
学会在 Modelsim 与 GHDL 下运行回归仿真
理解 CI（ciFlow.py + ci.do）通过 transcript 关键字判定通过/失败的逻辑
lectures.practice_task: 梳理一次完整的仿真运行步骤；说明若要新增一个测试台文件需修改 config.tcl 的哪几处；并解释 ciFlow.py 中 “###ERROR###” 与 “SIMULATIONS COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY” 两个判据各自的作用。

lectures.title: IP 打包与 Vivado 集成
lectures.topic: 讲解如何用 PsiIpPackage（scripts/package.tcl）把 HDL 打包成 Vivado IP：加入源文件与 psi_common 库、声明 GUI 参数页、设置 m_axis 可选端口的启用条件、目标器件与综合设置。
learning_goals: 理解 package.tcl 的打包流程与 PsiIpPackage 命令序列
理解源文件、psi_common 库与驱动文件如何被加入 IP
理解可选端口/接口基于 Output_g 的条件启用机制
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 package.tcl，写出一个 IP 打包步骤清单（初始化→加源→加库→加驱动→加 GUI 参数→设置可选端口→package_ip），并解释为何 m_axis 端口只在 Output_g=="AXIS" 时出现。

units.title: 进阶：核心模块与数据通路

lectures.title: 整体架构与数据流
lectures.topic: 自顶向下讲解 IP 核整体架构与数据流：s00_axi（从机，配置）→ RegTable RAM → 核心 FSM → m00_axi（主机，读寄存器）→ FIFO → AXIS 或 AXIMM 输出，结合 Documentation 的架构图。
learning_goals: 建立完整数据通路的心智模型
理解触发/超时如何启动一次读周期
理解 AXIS 与 AXIMM 两种输出模式在数据流上的差异
lectures.practice_task: 画一张数据流框图，标出 Trig/DoneIrq、s00_axi、RegTable、核心 FSM、m00_axi、FIFO 与输出端口的关系，并用一句话描述一次完整读周期的流程。

lectures.title: 寄存器映射与配置表
lectures.topic: 讲解寄存器映射：definitions_pkg.vhd 中的地址常量（Ctrl/RegCnt/RdData/RdLast/Level 与 MemOffs），对照 Documentation 寄存器表，理解每个寄存器的位含义与读写模式。
learning_goals: 掌握配置寄存器与内存映射表的布局
理解 Ena/RegCnt/RdData/RdLast/Level 各寄存器的作用与读写模式
理解 Addr[] 表从 0x20 开始的内存区与 MemOffs 的关系
lectures.practice_task: 用表格列出所有寄存器的地址、名称、模式、位宽与用途；并指出 AXIMM 模式下为何“读 RdData 会弹出 FIFO、因此要先读 RdLast”。

lectures.title: 核心 FSM：双进程状态机
lectures.topic: 深入 axi_mm_reader.vhd 的核心状态机：双进程（two-process）方法、状态 Idle/ReadAddr/SetCmd/ApplyCmd/WaitDone 的转换与每态动作、DoneCnt 计数与 Last 检测。
learning_goals: 掌握双进程（combinatorial + sequential）RTL 编码风格
理解 FSM 如何遍历 RegTable 逐个发起读取
理解 DoneCnt 计数与组合逻辑 Last 检测的作用
lectures.practice_task: 画出 FSM 状态转移图并标注转移条件；解释在 ApplyCmd_s 等待 AxiM_CmdRd_Rdy 时 RamAddr 已经自增到下一项的设计意图。

lectures.title: 触发与超时机制
lectures.topic: 讲解读周期启动机制：Trig 脉冲触发与 TimeoutCkCycles_g 超时自动触发、Enable 的门控、超时计数器的复位以及如何用超时实现周期性读取。
learning_goals: 理解触发与超时两种启动方式
理解 Enable 门控与禁用时立即回到 Idle 的行为
理解如何仅用超时（不接 Trig）实现周期性读取
lectures.practice_task: 阅读组合进程中 Trigger/TimeoutCnt/Enable 段落，说明“Enable=0 时的行为”与“读周期进行中 Trig 被忽略”分别由哪些代码保证；并计算 ClkFrequencyHz=100MHz、TimeoutUs_g=100 时 TimeoutCkCycles_c 的值（参照 wrapper 常量）。

lectures.title: AXI 从机配置接口（wrapper）
lectures.topic: 讲解 wrapper 中 s00_axi 从机一侧：psi_common_axi_slave_ipif 把 AXI 事务解码为寄存器读/写（reg_rd/reg_wr/reg_wdata/reg_rdata）与内存读/写（mem_addr/mem_wr/mem_wdata/mem_rdata），并映射到 Enable/RegCount/RegTable。
learning_goals: 理解 AXI 从机 IPIC 接口与寄存器/内存解码
理解 RegCount、Enable 如何从 reg_wdata 的位字段提取
理解 mem_addr 如何换算成 RegTable 索引（含字节地址右移）
lectures.practice_task: 追踪一次“软件写 RegCount”与一次“软件写 RegTable 某项”的完整路径（从 s00_axi 信号到核心端口），说明 reg_wdata(RegIdx_RegCnt_c) 与 mem_addr(log2ceil(MaxRegCount_g)+1 downto 2) 的换算含义。

lectures.title: AXI 主机读取通路
lectures.topic: 讲解 wrapper 中 m00_axi 主机一侧：psi_common_axi_master_simple 的实例化（只读不写、CmdRd 单拍事务、最大打开事务数、数据 FIFO 深度），以及核心如何用 CmdRd/RdDat 通道与之配合。
learning_goals: 理解 AXI 主机 IPIC 命令/数据接口
理解只读配置与单拍读取（AxiMaxBeats_g=1、CmdRd_Size="1"）
理解命令与数据通道的握手机制对吞吐/背压的影响
lectures.practice_task: 对照核心 FSM 的 SetCmd_s/ApplyCmd_s，描述一次“发命令→等 Rdy→收数据”的时序；并解释 DataFifoDepth_g 与 AxiMaxOpenTrasactions_g 对读取吞吐与背压的影响。

lectures.title: 输出模式与 FIFO/RegTable 存储
lectures.topic: 讲解两种输出模式的实现：psi_common_sync_fifo 缓存读取值（含 Last 位）、psi_common_tdp_ram 存放 RegTable；wrapper 中 g_axis/g_naxis generate 块在 AXIS（直接驱动 m_axis）与 AXIMM（把 FIFO 映射到 RdData/RdLast 寄存器）之间切换。
learning_goals: 理解同步 FIFO 的宽度（32+1 位含 Last）与深度（MaxRegCount×MinBuffers）
理解 generate 块的条件综合（g_axis vs g_naxis）
理解 AxiS_Level 与 Level 寄存器、AXIMM 下 AxiS_Rdy 的接法
lectures.practice_task: 解释 FIFO 的 InData(32)=Last 与 OutData(32)=AxiS_Last 的对应关系；并说明在 AXIMM 模式下 AxiS_Rdy 为何接到 reg_rd(RegIdx_RdData_c)（即软件读 RdData 弹出 FIFO）。

units.title: 专家：驱动、测试、打包与二次开发

lectures.title: C 软件驱动
lectures.topic: 讲解 Vitis/嵌入式侧的 C 驱动 drivers/axi_mm_reader：基于 Xil_In32/Xil_Out32 的寄存器访问、使能/寄存器表配置/Level 查询、单条与整包 FIFO 读取、错误码与“先读 RdLast 再读 RdData”约定，以及 Makefile/MDD/tcl 的驱动集成。
learning_goals: 掌握驱动 API（SetEnable/GetEnable/SetRegTable/GetLevel/ReadFifoEntry/ReadFifoPacket）
理解错误码（IpMustBeDisabled/FifoIsEmpty/NoCompletePacketInFifo）的含义与触发条件
理解驱动函数与寄存器映射、IP 使能状态的对应关系
lectures.practice_task: 编写一段示例代码：禁用 IP→用 SetRegTable 配置地址数组→使能→触发后用 ReadFifoPacket 读取一个完整包并打印；并说明 SetRegTable 为何要求 IP 处于禁用状态。

lectures.title: 测试台架构与用例
lectures.topic: 讲解 top_tb.vhd 的自校验测试台：AXI 主/从 BFM、基于 OutputType_g 的双模式、6 组激励/响应用例（单次读、缓冲双读、超时、禁用、背压、单寄存器四次读）及 CheckResults 等过程。
learning_goals: 理解自校验测试台结构（激励进程 p_control + 从机仿真进程 p_spi）
理解用 StimCase/RespCase 同步两个进程的握手机制
理解 AXIS 与 AXIMM 两条校验路径（CheckResultsAxiS/CheckResultsAxiMM）
lectures.practice_task: 选择“缓冲双读”用例，逐步说明 p_control 与 p_spi 如何通过 StimCase/RespCase 配合，并指出该用例验证了 IP 的什么行为（缓冲、数据不丢失）。

lectures.title: 参数化与 GUI 配置
lectures.topic: 讲解 IP 的参数化与 GUI：package.tcl 中声明的可配置参数（AxiSlaveAddrWidth_g/ClkFrequencyHz/TimeoutUs_g/MaxRegCount_g/MinBuffers_g/Output_g）及取值范围/下拉选项，xgui 的 init_gui 与 update/validate/update_MODEL 回调。
learning_goals: 理解各 generic 参数对硬件的影响（地址宽度、超时、缓冲深度、输出类型）
理解 GUI 参数与 VHDL generic 的映射机制（update_MODELPARAM_VALUE）
理解参数范围与下拉约束的设置方式
lectures.practice_task: 列一张“参数→默认值→范围→作用”表；并解释为何 s00_axi 地址宽度需满足 ceil(log2(MaxRegisters×4+32))（提示：寄存器区 + RegTable 内存区共享地址空间）。

lectures.title: IP-XACT 打包产物与 Block Design 集成
lectures.topic: 讲解 IP-XACT 打包与 Block Design 集成：bd/bd.tcl 中 init/pre_propagate/propagate 对 AXI4 ID_WIDTH 在主/从接口上的自动传播，以及 MDD/tcl 声明驱动用于 Vitis BSP 生成 xparameters.h。component.xml 内部细节为待确认。
learning_goals: 理解 Vivado IP-XACT 组件描述（component.xml）在打包流程中的作用（细节待确认）
理解 BD 钩子如何在不同时机自动传递 AXI4 ID_WIDTH
理解驱动如何在 Vitis BSP 中被识别并生成 xparameters.h
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 bd.tcl，说明 pre_propagate（针对 master）与 propagate（针对 slave）分别在什么时机修改 ID_WIDTH；并追踪 axi_mm_reader.tcl 的 generate 过程会向 xparameters.h 写入哪些字段（NUM_INSTANCES/DEVICE_ID/C_BASEADDR/C_HIGHADDR）。

lectures.title: 二次开发实践：扩展该 IP
lectures.topic: 二次开发实践：如何端到端扩展该 IP——新增配置寄存器（definitions_pkg + wrapper 解码 + 文档）、调整输出/缓冲深度、为新增行为编写测试用例并加入 sim/config.tcl、同步更新 C 驱动。
learning_goals: 掌握从寄存器定义到硬件/文档/测试/驱动的端到端改动流程
理解一次扩展需要同步修改的多处文件与潜在风险
具备安全扩展 IP（含仿真验证）的能力
lectures.practice_task: 设计一个“读取内部状态”的小扩展（例如新增一个只读寄存器反映 DoneCnt/Level），写出需要修改的文件清单与每个文件的关键改动点，并在 top_tb 中规划一个验证用例、在 config.tcl 中确认无需新增文件。
